Tests if multiplying `Number.NaN` by `+0` results in `NaN` using an assertion.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of the multiplication operation involving `Number.NaN` and `+0` in JavaScript. It uses the `assert` function to verify that the result of multiplying `Number.NaN` by `+0` is `NaN`, which is expected to evaluate to `true` when passed to the `isNaN` function. This test ensures that the JavaScript engine correctly handles arithmetic operations involving `NaN`, which is a special numeric value representing "Not-a-Number." The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific arithmetic behavior in JavaScript.
